content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward methods like ICM and RND, which reward agents for exploring uncertain state space, can be repurposed to design a reciprocal intrinsic reward mechanism for A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocols where agents reward each other for sharing surprising, unexperienced knowledge rather than just task-aligned information. This reciprocal curiosity reward creates a self-organizing collective exploration cycle that outperforms static A2A communication rules in open-ended active learning tasks.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
